title: Pong Pioneer: Ted Dabney's Gaming Legacy
story: Beep, boop, ping! The sounds of early video games echo through time, thanks to pioneers like Ted Dabney. Born in San Francisco on May 2, 1937, Dabney would grow up to revolutionize entertainment. As an engineer and co-founder of Atari, he helped create Pong, the iconic table tennis game that launched the video game industry. title: Peruvian Victory at Sea
story: Cannons boomed and ships clashed as Peruvian defenders bravely fought off a Spanish fleet on May 2, 1866. This fierce naval battle took place at Callao, Peru's main port. The Peruvians, using powerful shore batteries, successfully repelled the Spanish attackers. Their victory boosted national pride and marked the end of Spain's attempts to regain control in South America.
activity_question: Where did this naval battle take place?
bonus_fact: Callao remains Peru's chief seaport to this day.

title: A Dazzling New Stage in Paris
story: Lights! Music! Action! In 1869, Paris got a thrilling new place for entertainment. The Folies Trévise opened its doors on May 2, bringing opera to the City of Light. This fancy theater would later become the famous Folies Bergère, known for its glitzy cabaret shows. It was the start of something magical in the heart of France.
activity_question: What type of performances did the Folies Trévise originally showcase?
bonus_fact: The Folies Bergère became so popular that its name inspired similar theaters around the world.

title: Oklahoma's Wild West Beginnings
story: Yeehaw! The Wild West got a new frontier in 1890 when the Territory of Oklahoma was born on May 2. This vast land of prairies and cowboys wasn't a state yet—that wouldn't happen until 1907. For 17 years, Oklahoma Territory was a place of adventure, with settlers rushing to claim land and build new lives.
activity_question: What was created in 1890?
follow_up_question: How many years was Oklahoma a territory before becoming a state?
